sanskrit:
रागी कर्मफलप्रेप्सुर्लुब्धो हिंसात्मकोऽशुचिः।
हर्षशोकान्वितः कर्ता राजसः परिकीर्तितः॥
transliteration: rāgī karma-phala-prepsur lubdho hiṁsātmako 'śuciḥ | harṣa-śokānvitaḥ kartā rājasaḥ parikīrtitaḥ ||
literal_meaning: The doer who is full of attachment, desirous of the fruits of action, greedy, of harmful nature, impure, subject to joy and sorrow, is declared to be of the rajasic nature.
contextual_meaning: Krishna describes the rajasic worker: driven by craving for results, emotionally volatile, morally flexible, and trapped in an endless cycle of elation and despair tied to success or failure.
psychological_state: High-arousal, reward-dependent motivation with fragile self-worth; oscillates between grandiosity and worthlessness based on external outcomes.
trigger_scenario: Checking your phone every minute after posting on social media, feeling euphoric with likes and crushed by silence, then scrolling others’ posts with secret resentment.
anchor_text: Work with steady mind, not swayed by gain or loss.
